facebook.texto: The first digestive support made for GLP-1 constipation. Supports healthy bile flow — the natural lubricant your colon lost when your appetite dropped. ✅ Gentle daily regularity — no cramping, no urgency ✅ No sludge, no disgusting liquids, no nuclear options ✅ Nightly capsule — works while you sleep ✅ Designed for Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro & Zepbound 60-day money back guarantee → Try Omyo risk-free https://omyohealth.com/glp1s

analise_criativa.avatar: White woman in her 30s-40s, brown hair, casual t-shirt, sitting at a kitchen or dining table in a home environment. Relaxed, conversational tone, speaking directly to camera.
analise_criativa.editing: Raw vertical video shot on smartphone, natural home lighting, minimal editing. Text overlay visible at top of frame in early seconds. Authentic low-production UGC aesthetic typical of TikTok-style content.
analise_criativa.observation: The ad explicitly names brand products — Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro, Zepbound — and promotes a named product (Omyo). This is a supplement/digestive support product targeting GLP-1 users rather than a VSL-driving weight loss creative. The page name 'Hannah Martin' suggests a creator or persona account. The text overlay 'ANAL FISSURE FROM GLP-1 CONSTIPATION' visible at 3s is highly attention-grabbing but also potentially sensitive or platform-risky.
analise_criativa.highlight: Strong scroll-stop potential from the taboo/pain-point angle around GLP-1 side effects. The constipation hook is a real underserved conversation among Ozempic/Mounjaro users. The UGC format with a relatable woman speaking casually builds trust quickly. Worth testing a similar avatar and hook around digestive pain points in a VSL funnel context.

facebook.texto: You didn't fail the diet. The diet never addressed what was actually broken. ⠀ Because if your metabolism's signaling is off — hunger doesn't stop when it should, blood sugar spikes and crashes all day, and your body holds onto fat like a famine is coming — no calorie deficit fixes that. ⠀ This is the part the weight loss industry skips. ⠀ And it's why millions of people are eating well, moving their bodies, doing everything "right" — and nothing moves. ⠀

analise_criativa.avatar: Young Latina woman in her kitchen, wearing a grey tank top, shown in multiple states — covering her face (before), drinking from a cup, and standing next to what appears to be a product on the counter. Text overlays reference 'after two weeks, 13 lbs' and 'I'm 45'.
analise_criativa.editing: Vertical mobile-first format, natural kitchen lighting, casual handheld feel typical of TikTok-style UGC. On-screen text captions overlay key claims. Low production value designed to feel authentic and organic.
analise_criativa.observation: The 30s frame shows what may be a product container on the counter — this edges toward product visibility and should be flagged for review before scaling. The brand ColonBroom is a known advertiser so this may be intentional brand exposure.
analise_criativa.highlight: Strong scroll-stop potential: the before/after framing with a relatable real woman in an everyday setting (kitchen), combined with a specific result ('13 lbs, 2 weeks') and age anchor ('I'm 45') creates immediate curiosity and identification for the core target demo. The ad copy angle — 'you didn't fail the diet' — is a high-empathy hook worth testing as a headline variant.

facebook.texto: This is how I regained the confidence I had when I was young after years of being overweight. I want to tell you about the moment I realized something had fundamentally changed. I was at a restaurant I'd been going to for fifteen years. Same maitre d'. Same table they used to hold for me without being asked. The kind of place where the sommelier remembers your name and the chef sends out a little something extra just because you walked in. I stood at the host stand for four minutes.

analise_criativa.avatar: Woman (Megan Hartwell) in what appears to be a kitchen or home environment, positioned as a relatable middle-aged woman sharing a personal weight loss transformation story with strong emotional narrative
analise_criativa.editing: Static image with lifestyle/personal photo aesthetic, warm tones, natural home setting — mimics organic social post rather than polished ad creative
analise_criativa.observation: The ad copy uses a long-form storytelling hook (restaurant scene, 15-year relationship with the venue, being unrecognized) which is an unusually sophisticated DR narrative for a supplement ad — more editorial in tone than typical weight loss ads. No product is shown in the image. The page name has no brand attached, increasing perceived authenticity.
analise_criativa.highlight: The storytelling angle is highly differentiated — using social invisibility and loss of identity as the emotional hook rather than vanity or health. This type of narrative can generate strong scroll-stops and emotional resonance with women 45+ in US market. Worth testing against standard transformation hooks.
